key_insight: Prototype-based alignment enhances the data efficiency of fine-tuning molecular foundation models for improved material property predictions.
engineering_relevance: The proposed method addresses the challenge of generalisation in machine learning interatomic potentials for chemically diverse materials.
method: Identifies local structural similarities between source and target domains by grouping atoms with analogous chemical environments.
Aligns each target-domain atom's energy contribution to its source-domain prototype.
Encourages effective reuse of learned interactions and improves generalisation without restrictive assumptions on target chemistry.
evidence_signal: The approach demonstrates consistent improvements in predictive accuracy in low-data regimes, achieving up to 18% reduction in energy MAE compared to standard fine-tuning baselines.
watch_item: Potential overfitting in data-scarce regimes despite method improvements.
paper_title: Prototype-Guided Latent Alignment for Data-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Molecular Foundation Models
